title: Quizer Demo
description: A small sample bank to show how Quizer works. Replace it with your own.
author: Quizer

questions.prompt: What is the capital of Japan?
questions.explanation: Tokyo has been Japan's capital since 1868, when the emperor moved there from Kyoto.

questions.prompt: Which of these is the longest river?
questions.explanation: The Nile is generally cited as the longest river at about 6,650 km, though the Amazon is a close contender by some measures.

questions.prompt: In which year did the first human land on the Moon?
questions.explanation: Apollo 11 landed on 20 July 1969; Neil Armstrong stepped onto the surface that day.

questions.prompt: What is the chemical symbol for gold?
questions.explanation: Au comes from the Latin 'aurum'. Ag (argentum) is silver.

questions.prompt: Which of the following are noble gases? (Select all that apply.)
questions.explanation: Helium, neon, and argon are noble gases (group 18). Oxygen is not.

questions.prompt: What gas do plants primarily absorb for photosynthesis?
questions.explanation: Plants take in CO₂ and release O₂ during photosynthesis.
The CPU is the primary component that executes instructions of a program.

questions.prompt: How many bits are in a byte?
questions.explanation: A byte is 8 bits, able to represent 256 distinct values.

questions.prompt: Which of these are programming languages? (Select all that apply.)
questions.explanation: Python and Rust are programming languages. HTML is a markup language; HTTP is a protocol.
